task_completion.description: Diagram contains horizontal swimlanes representing Academic Years 1-4.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for horizontal dividers, lanes, or containers clearly labeled with 'Year 1', 'Year 2', 'Year 3', and 'Year 4' organizing the diagram space.

task_completion.description: Courses are represented as nodes labeled with both Course Code and Name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for approximately 20 distinct shapes/boxes. Each box must contain text showing a course code (e.g., 'CS101') AND a course name. Ensure required courses like CS101, CS102, CS300, CS310, CS401, CS405, and CS490 are present.

task_completion.description: Nodes are color-coded into distinct colors based on course type (Core, Math/GenEd, AI/Specialization).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the fill colors of the course nodes. There should be at least three distinct background colors used across the nodes to differentiate the categories.

task_completion.description: Directed arrows connect the course nodes to show prerequisite dependencies.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for lines with arrowheads pointing from prerequisite courses to subsequent courses. Verify specific chains exist, such as CS101 -> CS102 -> CS201 -> CS202, or CS300 -> CS310 -> CS405.

task_completion.description: The diagram is saved as ai_curriculum_map.drawio in the ~/Diagrams/ folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the draw.io application window title bar showing 'ai_curriculum_map.drawio', or a file manager/terminal window showing this file inside the 'Diagrams' directory.

task_completion.description: The diagram is exported as a PDF named ai_curriculum_map.pdf in the ~/Diagrams/ folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file manager window or terminal showing 'ai_curriculum_map.pdf' in the 'Diagrams' directory, or a PDF viewer displaying the exported map.

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical diagramming tool (like draw.io) to create the map.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the draw.io/diagrams.net application interface with the diagram being actively edited, rather than a text editor writing raw XML.

integrity.description: The PDF was exported using the application's export functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Export as PDF' dialog, print dialog, or menu being accessed in the draw.io interface, proving it wasn't just a renamed file.

integrity.description: The agent opened or read the curriculum_data.csv file to obtain the course information.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a spreadsheet application, text editor, or terminal window displaying the contents of 'curriculum_data.csv' at some point in the trajectory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The curriculum data contains exactly 20 courses and 20 prerequisite edges. Required courses to look for: CS101, CS102, CS300, CS310, CS401, CS405, CS490. You must verify these specific prerequisite chains exist with directed arrows: 1) CS101 -> CS102 -> CS201 -> CS202. 2) MATH101 -> MATH102 -> MATH201. 3) CS300 -> CS310 -> CS405. The output files must be named 'ai_curriculum_map.drawio' and 'ai_curriculum_map.pdf' and located in '/home/ga/Diagrams/'.
